message:
fix(sync): drop lock before waking in MPSC try_recv, extract Notify baton-pass, simplify pool timeout

MPSC try_recv: restructured to drop the inner lock before waking the
next sender, preventing potential deadlock when the sender's wake
handler re-enters channel operations. The disconnected flag is now
read before dropping the lock to avoid a second atomic load. This
mirrors the same lock-then-drop-then-wake pattern already used in
poll_recv.

Notify: extracted the baton-pass logic from Drop into a shared
remove_and_baton_pass() method, which is now called from both the
Poll::Ready(()) path (when generation changes are detected while
in Waiting state) and the Drop impl. Previously the generation-change
path used a simpler cleanup() that did not perform baton passing,
which could lose notify_one tokens if a Notified future observed a
generation change and returned Ready without passing its token.

Pool acquire timeout: simplified the timeout match arm by replacing
match Ok/Err with an is_err() check, reducing nesting. The
wait_duration calculation and metrics recording remain identical.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
